title: Reconciliation runs as a harmonik workflow
Reconciliation workflows emit exactly one checkpoint commit
Bounded recursion follows from the verdict-only commit rule
At most one reconciliation workflow per target run (flock)
Lock acquisition and verdict-executed-commit are NOT atomic
Detectors and verdict-execution are NOT in the workflow library
Upgrade discipline — daemon code and library ship together
Action-mapping is the dispatch contract; taxonomy is detection
Auto-resolver categories MUST have a deterministic resolver
Detectors operate on runs, not beads
Ordering uses git DAG parentage and UUID v7, not wall clock
Cat 0 pre-check runs before any other detector
Post-`ready` Cat 0 does not transition daemon state
Investigator inputs are bound by snapshot token (logical view)
Investigator is an HC handler (canonical agent_type/role pair)
Investigator playbook per category (S01 YAML obligation)
Every reconciliation workflow declares a wall-clock budget
Budget exhaustion terminates with fallback verdict (5-step sequence kept as one bead per F8b)
Verdict vocabulary is the seven-value enum
Exactly one verdict event per reconciliation workflow
Verdict commit lands on investigator's task branch
Verdict emission via outcome envelope (HC-008 outcome_emitted)
Malformed-verdict handling produces a fallback escalation
Verdict execution is durable and idempotent
Daemon-side verdict-executor commits the verdict pair (7-step sequence kept as one bead per F8b)
Cat 3b re-execution retry cap (default N=5; cap → Cat 6b)
`reopen-bead` verdict triggers a new run on subsequent claim
Intra-run rollbacks keep the worktree and run_id
Reconciliation does NOT drive intra-run loops
Sensor: reconciliation-as-workflow uniqueness across daemon lifecycle
Sensor: evidence-corroboration guarantee across detector runs
Detector harness + cadence + corroboration (RC-010..RC-014, RC-019a, RC-020a, RC-020b)
Verdict + execution harness (RC-020..RC-027, RC-022a, RC-025a, RC-026a)
Crash-recovery suite (RC-031 — every detector + verdict-execution path)
